rows.notes: Topmost line reads 'Jospy Fines' (handwritten p looks like a descender). Pounds and shillings (14 and 7) align clearly in the left two of the three right-hand columns. Pence column is faint — there is a small mark but illegible; I set pence to 0 and note uncertainty about a small scribble to the right.
Clear reading. Amount printed as '00 10 0' in the three right-hand columns; columns are well aligned and values within valid ranges.

rows.description: Bursary & Impropris Colla Et Nota Donus
rows.notes: This centered/introductory line appears to be a section header (no amounts present). Handwriting is moderately clear but some words (Impropris/Donus) are abbreviated/uncertain.

Description and date legible. Numeric columns read as 18 | 17 | 6; alignment with vertical rules is clear and values pass validation. Minor uncertainty due to slight ink blur on the pence digit but 6 is the most plausible reading.
Similar format to the prior Bagley row. Columns clearly aligned as 22 | 17 | 6. Ink slightly lighter than row 4 but digits unambiguous.

rows.description: P. Question of Domiciny of misc & St Thomas 1722
rows.notes: Description is somewhat cramped and bracketed with the previous entries; the right-hand numbers read as 37 | 12 | 4. Moderate uncertainty because the bracketing suggests these lines are related and the exact wording of the description is partially abbreviated.

rows.description: Johns Lady Day & St John Bapt. 1724
rows.notes: Line with date/occasion follows the previous bracketed items but no monetary amounts are written on this specific line — appears to be a descriptive sub-item (no values present). The text is legible.
Clearly a centered account header introducing the following block. No amounts on the same line. Handwriting legible as a heading.

rows.description: ad first nat { p Aug: mi Southm. Et Morton }
rows.notes: Bracketed entry; description partly abbreviated ('Southm.' interpreted as Southm[pton?]). Amounts read clearly as 23 | 0 | 0 and align with the column rules.
Description legible (Latinized). Right-hand columns read 18 | 6 | 8 clearly; values within valid ranges. Slight ink blot on the '8' but alignment supports this reading.

rows.description: ad first annum { p Aug. Rd Edlington 3 part }
rows.notes: Handwriting of the descriptive portion is moderately unclear (Rd Edlington uncertain). Amount columns appear to show 28 | 5 | 0 but the shillings/pence area is slightly smeared making the pence uncertain; chose 0 as the clearest digit.
Single-word entry 'Stipend' with a clear amount to the right: 30 | 0 | 0. Alignment and digits are readable.

rows.description: ad first Sept { p A parte Rd Cudl }
rows.notes: Description is abbreviated and partly unclear ('Rd Cudl' tentative). Right-hand numeric grouping reads 10 | 9 | 2; pence digit is small but legible enough to choose 2.
A second occurrence of 'Stipend Et Officiariij'. Numerical columns again read 18 | 6 | 8; clear alignment supports this reading.

rows.description: ad first Mice { p Aug: mi. Rd Long W. }
rows.notes: Description is abbreviated and somewhat unclear ('Mice' and 'Rd Long W.' are transcribed as they appear). Right-hand amount reads 23 | 1 | 3; pence is small but legible in context with the column alignment.
Description partly abbreviated ('Guinecar' as transcribed). The numeric columns are clean: 24 | 5 | 0.

A subtotal/total is written below the block; the first number '269' and the shillings '18' are legible. The pence column is less crisp; interpreted as 10 based on alignment with the three right-hand rules and comparison with other totals on the page.

Latin heading 'Summa omnium receptorum' followed by a large total on the right. Pounds appears as 536, shillings 1 and pence 9 are plausible from the faint digits; ink here is lighter so reading is moderately uncertain.
Line labelled 'Deductorum' (deductions) with amounts to the right. The numbers 456 | 9 | 2 are reasonably clear and consistent with the visible figures; digits are darker than row 18.

Final balancing line (Latin) showing the remainder. The pounds '79' is clear; shillings and pence are less distinct — I read 11 | 6 but the pence digit in particular is faint and could be ambiguous (hence lower confidence).

rows.description: Nos quorum nomina subscripta sunt ... (long Latin paragraph)
rows.notes: Long explanatory Latin paragraph spans several lines; no monetary amounts on these narrative lines. Text is generally legible though some abbreviations occur; treated as header/narrative.
Year mark in left margin '1723' introducing the small list of petty payments below. No amounts on the same line.

rows.description: Pd for a Certificate &c to the Bishop
rows.notes: Small petty payment line in the lower left with amount read as 01 | 01 | 00; description is abbreviated and faint but 'certificate' and 'Bishop' are plausible reconstructions from the handwriting. Ink faint affecting confidence.

rows.description: Pd The Steward for a Certificate to the Visitors
rows.notes: Another petty payment line; the amount aligns as 01 | 01 | 00. Wording 'The Steward for a Certificate to the Visitors' is inferred from abbreviations and context; handwriting is fairly small and faded.

rows.description: Pd for entering the Visitors Injunctions in the Books
rows.notes: Small payment shown as 01 | 00 | 00. Description is abbreviated and the phrase 'entering the Visitors Injunctions' is a best reading of the cramped script; amount digits are clearer than the words.

rows.description: Pd for a man & one horse to the Visitors
rows.notes: Final small payment in this list reads 01 | 00 | 00. The description 'a man & one horse to the Visitors' is legible though compact; amount digits clear but small.
Signature/attestation block at the foot of the page. Multiple names appear; no monetary amounts. 'Matth.' and 'Rector' are clear; surname partly abbreviated/uncertain.
Additional signatories listed (names and abbreviations). No amounts. Handwriting is cursive but recognizable as signatures; some surnames are abbreviated which lowers confidence slightly.
